mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.13% suggests deep easing regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 50,000 net new jobs, signaling labor market weakness that precedes equity selloffs as investors anticipate recession risk and reduced corporate earnings growth over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50,000 jobs for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward SPX returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously signals weakening aggregate demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side fragility that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by more than 2% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 446,477 million dollars, HOUST at 1,174 thousand units
regime_hint: demand shock or early recession signal
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month moving average, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power that supports corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.5 index points
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, creating negative real rates that trigger equity repricing as investors demand higher risk premiums and expect future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during elevated unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, signaling robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors increase growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 16,270.4 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,000 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) growth slows below 1.0% month-over-month, jointly signaling consumer demand weakness that precedes equity market correction as investors reduce growth forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,000 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth is below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,174 thousand units, RSAFS at 446,477 million dollars
regime_hint: consumer demand shock or housing market contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 51% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.
